name: BELLINGHAUSEN RES (MIKE)
address: 2157 NW CASCADE VIEW DR, BEND, OR 97701

fr.elements: Main Building; Detached Structure; Landscaped Area
fr.recommendations: Improving the management of dense vegetation around both the main building and detached structure could enhance responder visibility and support efficient movement during emergency operations. Ensuring that side access points are kept open and easily navigable may facilitate quicker access and egress for emergency personnel, which is crucial in time-sensitive situations. Strategies such as identifying and maintaining clear secondary access routes could further support effective staging and navigation. Consistently monitored and trimmed landscaping would aid in maintaining unobstructed views of the property, potentially leading to a more coordinated and confident response during emergencies.

wildfire.conditions: Vegetation Adjacent to Structure; Scattered Trees; Limited Defensible Space; Dense Tree Canopy; Vegetated Perimeter; Tree Cluster; Vegetation Overhang; Vegetation Adjacent to Access Route; Dense Vegetation Corridor; Vegetation Between Structures
wildfire.recommendations: Enhancing defensible space by increasing separation between vegetation and structures and managing vegetation adjacent to structures could reduce exposure and improve property preparedness. Thinning scattered trees and the dense tree canopy may decrease fuel continuity and support long-term vegetation management. Creating breaks in the vegetated perimeter and reducing the density of vegetation corridors could assist in preventing fire from spreading towards structures. Addressing vegetation overhangs and clusters, particularly near buildings and along access routes, may support firefighter access and suppression efforts. Additionally, managing vegetation between structures can further support fire mitigation efforts and improve both immediate and long-term wildfire resilience. Ensuring regular maintenance of these areas through strategic vegetation management could enhance overall property safety and readiness.
